sections.title: Starting off
Talking to Dwarven Boatman

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Ho there, human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Ho there, [player name]! Want to take a ride with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are you going? Across the river?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: No no, that's what the ferryman is for! I'm going to Keldagrim, my home!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much will that cost me then?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: For a human like you, I can do it for free!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a deal!

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Excellent! I'm just waiting for my ship to arrive and then we can go.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Mind, this trip could take a few minutes! Are you sure you're ready to go as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm ready and don't mind it taking a few minutes.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Well, let's not waste any more time then!

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Look, there's the statue of King Alvis, guarding the entrance to Keldagrim!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Amazing! And we're going to go straight past it?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Er, hold on! There's something wrong!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Black Guard Berserker
lines.text: Halt! By order of the Black Guard, you are under arrest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?? I didn't do anything! It wasn't me!

lines.speaker: Black Guard Berserker
lines.text: Come with us, human! Commander Veldaban wants a word with you!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Identify yourself, human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I err, I'm [player name]. I was told I was under arrest. I had a little accident on the way in here.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Ah, so you're the one who knocked over the statue of our glorious king!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose so... I mean, I didn't do it on purpose or anything...

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Ah, don't worry about it!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: We haven't been ruled by a king for many a year... that statue was long overdue for replacement anyway. You're not under arrest, I merely wanted to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: But let me introduce myself. I am Veldaban, son of Dondakan. I am the Commander of the Black Guard in Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan, you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped your father in the mines south of the city.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Ah, so you are the one! I heard you banished a demon by defeating its avatar, most impressive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan, you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm helping your father in the mines south of the city.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Ah, he is forever trying to break open that rock of his, until his death I sometimes think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't think it's possible?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Perhaps, human, perhaps... but if even our multicannon does not dent it, what chance is there really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, ehm... nice to meet you, Veldaban.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Now listen carefully. I have a task for you.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: I want you to go and see the local sculptor and help him build a new statue.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Will you do this for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I can't be bothered.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: You destroy our statue and you cannot be bothered to help rebuild it? By Guthix, you are rude... even for a human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I will do this.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: You are a good human, [player name].

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Now, I need you to go to Blasidar, he is a sculptor who lives in Keldagrim-East.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest I'm not much of a sculptor myself.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Oh, don't worry! I just need you to help him out while he rebuilds the statue.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: I'm sure there are many tasks that he needs doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have time right now, I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Sure, sure! Come back when you need to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I prefer the mines to the city.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Well, suit yourself then!

sections.title: Talking to Veldaban again after agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: How are you getting on with rebuilding our statue, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't really done much yet...

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Get back to it as soon as you can, please!

sections.title: Talking to Riki before agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, can you help me?

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Talk... to Blasidar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No, I was wondering if you could help me.

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Talk... to Blasidar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, alright.

sections.title: Talking to Blasidar the sculptor
Talking to Blasidar after declining to help

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you needed doing again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, are you Blasidar, the famous sculptor?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Why, yes, yes, that I am!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well, I'm [player name]. Veldaban of the Black Guard sent me to you, you see. Said you might need some help with the statue.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Oh, I don't need an assistant sculptor, really, I can do just fine on my own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not a sculptor.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: And I've also got a model here, you see, to base my statue on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not a model!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: But come to think of it, I do need some errands run. Yes, quite important errands too.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Yes, you will do just fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ehm, right, okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did you need doing?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Well, see... the Consortium has commisioned me to rebuild the statue exactly the way it was.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: But the statue itself has broken into a million pieces... and we dwarves aren't very good painters, you know, so we don't have any paintings of it either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a very artistic race, then, are you?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: No, that's not what I said, I said we're not very good painters.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: We're excellent with, for example, stone. Oh yes, quite good if I say so myself.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: But I digress... simply speaking, I need some help with getting some items that I can base the new statue on.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Will you be able to help me with this, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I can't be bothered.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: How rude is that? I don't think the Black Guard will be very pleased with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I will do this.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Excellent, excellent! Now what I need is the following...

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: I will need a pair of boots, the most exquisite boots you can find. The more they look like the original the better, but I don't think you'll be able to find an exact match.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: For the torso, I need clothes that look like King Alvis' clothes. I'm not sure where you would be able to find that, but I'm sure you'll manage.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Also, I need a battleaxe. Now this is particularly important. You are in luck, however, as I believe King Alvis' original axe still survives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, so I need a pair of boots, a piece of clothing and a battleaxe. Is that all?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: For now, [player name], for now! I wish you good luck, and hurry back with the items!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... wait, wait, how am I supposed to find all this?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: I don't know! You're supposed to find that out for me!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: But it's a beautiful city, Keldagrim is, I'm sure you'll see many things while you do these tasks!

sections.title: Clothes fit for a king
Talking to Vermundi

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Welcome to my clothes stall, can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm looking for some special clothes.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh, that sounds interesting, what's this about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm helping the sculptor to rebuild the statue of King Alvis. But he wants to know what his clothes looked like.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh dear, that statue is in a terrible mess, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I could hear the tumult from over here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the clothes, do you have any similar clothes?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh no, no, I can't quite recall what the clothes looked like. But I know someone who can!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Well, I'm sure Hugi the librarian in West can help you along.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: West?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Keldagrim-West, dear... on the other side of the Kelda, towards the north end of the city.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I'm sure he can show you the exact designs of the clothes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need the actual clothes themselves, not just the designs.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh, just bring the designs to me then, I'll fix you up some nice clothes!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Sounds like a fun little thing to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks a lot, I'll check out the library!

sections.title: Talking to Hugi

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Welcome to the Keldagrim library, human traveller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about King Alvis's clothes?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: His clothes? How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm helping Blasidar the sculptor to rebuild the statue of King Alvis, but he needs to know the design of his clothes.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I see, I see.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: There is a book, somewhere in the library, that may help you. It is the 'Scholars Guide to Dwarven Costumes' and it contains the designs of the costumes of a lot of old Kings and dwarven dignitaries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like just the thing. Where exactly could I find it?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Let me think... I believe it is on the top shelf of one of the bookcases in the library, because it is such an old book.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you be any more specific? Upstairs, downstairs?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I don't remember, sorry. You will have to try several bookcases. You will probably need the ladders, tall a human though you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks, I'll have a look.

sections.title: Retrieving the book
Trying to pick up the book off the ground while already having a copy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bah. Annoying dwarven ladders, they're much too flimsy.

sections.title: Returning to Vermundi

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Welcome to my clothes stall, can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, about those special clothes again...

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh yes, did you get those designs from the librarian?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't actually spoken to the librarian yet, let me do that now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have them yet, but I can get them for you. The librarian has a book.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: A book, how wonderful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have a book right here, have a look at it!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Oh, I see, I see!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I can definitely help you with this.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I only have one small problem, you see... I've run out of fuel for my spinning machine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it run on?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Steam, of course!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: But I've used up all my coal and wood and I don't have the time to leave my stall to get them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll get them for you. Let's see... some coal and some logs. Shouldn't be too hard.

sections.title: Lighting the spinning machine
Attempting to light the spinning machine after it is already lit
Talking to Vermundi after giving her the book

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Welcome to my clothes stall, can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, about those special clothes again...

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Well, like I said, I can't do anything really without my spinning machine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, and what did you need again to start the engine?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Some coal and logs should do it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got them right here!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Then put them in the machine dear!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Well, the spinning machine is all ready now, except that it needs to be powered up.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: As soon as it's up and running, I can spin your clothes for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does that work?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Why, you light the logs of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'll do it! Just a moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have those clothes now?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Yes, I can spin them for you, but I will have to ask you for a small payment of money for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, after all that work?

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I'm sorry, but the cloth to spin this is expensive. But for you, I can make the clothes for a nominal fee of 200 credits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll pay, I'll pay.

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: Wonderful, let me get started then!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: There we go! And look how beautiful these clothes look!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't pay, that's outrageous!

lines.speaker: Vermundi
lines.text: I'm sorry you feel that way, I was looking forward to making these clothes.

sections.title: Attempting to pick up exquisite clothes when you already have some
Wearing the clothes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These clothes are too small for me, I might ruin them if I put them on. I'd better take them to the sculptor instead.

sections.title: Boots fit for a king
Talking to Saro

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Welcome to my store, human! Are you interested in buying anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm looking for a pair of special boots.

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Boots, you say? What kind of boots?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The best you have! Boots fit for a king!

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Hmm, let's see... Well, I've got this pair of very exclusive boots. They're not armoured, but they look nice.

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Or rather, I had. I'm afraid I sold them, come to think of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you don't have anything else?

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: I don't normally stock boots, no, nothing that's appropriate for... what did you say? A king?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, tell me! Who did you sell the boots to?

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Old chap here in the west part of town, his name is Dromund. He's quite well known, not only for his wealth, but also for his... pecularities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What house does he live in?

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Go out the door, and head north. You'll have the headquarters of the Black Guard on your left and the bank on your right. Keep heading north and you'll find Dromund's house on your right. Can't miss it!

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: And if you're going to try to get the boots off him, good luck. You'll need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Saro again

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: Welcome to my store, human! Are you interested in buying anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm looking for a pair of special boots.

lines.speaker: Saro
lines.text: I thought I already told you where to get them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes. The old dwarf north of the bank has them, I remember now.

sections.title: Trying to take the boots before talking to Dromund
Talking to Dromund

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: You're trespassing, get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Calm down! I just want to ask you a few questions.

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Are you with the Black Guard or something?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: No, you wouldn't be, you're just a human.

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: What do you want from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I went to the Quality Armour shop. The proprietor told me you have just recently purchased a pair of boots from him.

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Right, and nice they look too on my display tables.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not actually using them then? Might I be able to buy them off you?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Good one, human!

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: They're mine and the last thing I'll do is part with them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me, how much will you accept for these boots?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Get out you pesky human! The boots are mine and you're not having them!

sections.title: Talking to Dromund again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you don't want to give me those boots?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Get out of here before I get one of my servants to throw you out!

sections.title: Taking the left boot

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: What are you doing there?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing...

sections.title: Taking the right boot

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Get your hands off that, you dirty human!

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Stay away from there! Who do you think you are, coming into my house like that?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: What are you doing, casting spells in my house? Get out!

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Hey! Is there someone at my window?

lines.speaker: Dromund
lines.text: Scram before I get the Black Guard to arrest you!

sections.title: Trying to pick up the left or right boot while already having the exquisite boots
Wearing the boots

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, wear just one boot? Besides, the boot is too precious for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't wear these boots, I don't want anything to happen to them before I bring them to the sculptor.

sections.title: Dropping the boots
An axe fit for a king
Talking to Santiri

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Welcome, human, to the Quality Weapons Shop! Can I interest you in a purchase?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm looking for a particular battleaxe.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Fancy a particular colour then? Blue is quite popular at the moment, and red is on the rise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, actually, I'm looking for King Alvis' axe.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: The King's axe!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: What would a human want with it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been tasked by Veldaban, Commander of the Black Guard in Keldagrim, to assist in every way possible with the rebuilding of King Alvis' statue.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Oh yes, the statue... a most unfortunate incident.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Not that I'm entirely sure that it was merely an accident, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I'm not entirely sure what I mean, human. But the axe, you say, what exactly did you need it for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give me the axe already, I know you have it!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I'll give it to you, embedded into your head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had just about enough of this! Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Blasidar the sculptor needs it for his statue. He wants a model for the axe, you see, and he believes the original axe still survives.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: And so it does!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: It's been in my family's care for a few generations now, after it has been lost for a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to it?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I do not know how it got lost, but my great-grandfather found it at the banks of the Kelda, further downstream from the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I see it?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: There is not much left of it, to be honest. The blade has almost completely rusted away, and the sapphires on the hilt were either stolen or were washed away by the river before it was found.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I doubt it is useful to Blasidar in this state, master sculptor though he is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure this is the real axe then?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Not entirely, no, but it matches the description well enough, and the holes for the sapphires are still clearly visible on the hilt. But since no one has a similar claim, I don't really care. It's genuine to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is tedious, I've lost interest.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I'll get back to work then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I can repair the axe?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: On your own? I doubt it. The sapphires, perhaps, if you are a skilled enough crafter. But the blade...

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Your feeble smithing skills will be of no use. No, you will need expert help on this.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: You're a decent smith, but this is far beyond your capabilities.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Formidable though your smithing skills are, this is beyond your capabilities.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Clearly you are a master smith, perhaps one of the best human smiths in all of Gielinor. Yet this is beyond even you.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: What you need is the skills of an Imcando dwarf... but they must all be dead by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could try and find one?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Yes, perhaps, perhaps...

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Perhaps the librarian, just due east of here, can help you.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Yes, I like the sound of this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I know where I can find an Imcando dwarf!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He actually made a sword for me a while ago. I'm sure I can get him to help me again.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Really? That is amazing news.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He lives on a cliff at the very south end of Asgarnia, just south of Port Sarim. I can visit him again if you like.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Excellent! You can take a cart to Ice Mountain from the station here, that should help you get there faster.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I will entrust you with King Alvis' battleaxe, then, human.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Help to restore it to its former glory!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: And please ask Blasidar to return it to me after he's done with it, I would sure love to see the restored blade again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I will not disappoint you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really care for it after all.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Then stop wasting my time.

sections.title: Talking to Santiri again
Trying to pick up the axe while already having one
Trying to wield the axe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should be very careful with this battleaxe, it's almost a museum piece! I don't want to damage it by wielding it.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Welcome, human, to the Quality Weapons Shop! Can I interest you in a purchase?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm looking for a particular battleaxe.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? You still have it, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I was just wondering what I should be doing with this piece of junk.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Piece of junk??

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I thought you were going to put sapphires on it and bring it to an Imcando dwarf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, of course, that's what I was going to do!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? You still have it, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... sort of... ehm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I lost it!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: At least you are honest, human.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: I found it, actually, after you lost it. I'm not happy with giving it to you again, but the rebuilding of the statue is important.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Be careful with it this time, alright?

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? You still have it, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and I've crafted some sapphires on the hilt, look!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: That's a great start! Now if you can find an Imcando dwarf so the blade can be repaired, that would be even better!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? You still have it, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and I got Thurgo the Imcando dwarf to fix it up nice and proper! Look!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Oh, that's beautiful! All it needs now is three sapphires crafted onto the hilt.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? You still have it, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and look, it's all fixed up now! I got Thurgo the Imcando dwarf to fix the blade, and I've crafted three sapphires onto the hilt.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: That is amazing! Absolutely astounding work!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Go and give Blasidar a hand with it then, and make sure to tell him to send it back to me when he's done with it!

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: Ah, you mean King Alvis' axe? Didn't you give it to Blasidar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, actually, I was just wondering if he returned it to you yet.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: No, I believe he's still using it.

lines.speaker: Santiri
lines.text: But thanks for your help with restoring the axe to its former glory!

sections.title: Talking to Hugi (with The Knight's Sword incomplete)

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Welcome to the Keldagrim library, human traveller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me find an Imcando dwarf?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: An Imcando dwarf, you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I need the help of an Imcando dwarf to repair an ancient battleaxe.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I see, I see.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Ah, I'm afraid I can't help you here, Keldagrim lost contact with that particular tribe of dwarves a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there no one else who can help me?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I suppose you could try Reldo, in the palace of Varrock. He is a most capable human librarian and knows quite a bit about us dwarves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think he can help me?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: He lives quite a good deal closer to where the Imcando used to live than we do, so it is not unlikely. Tell him Hugi the librarian sent you and I'm sure he'll be happy to help.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: And take a cart to Ice Mountain from the station in the east side of the city. It'll help you get to Varrock faster.

sections.title: Talking to Reldo (with The Knight's Sword incomplete)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Imcando dwarves?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The Imcando dwarves, you say? Let's see...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, yes... For many hundreds of years, they were the world's most skilled smiths. They used secret smithing knowledge passed down from generation to generation.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Unfortunately, about a century ago, the once thriving group was wiped out during the barbarian invasions of that time.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I believe a few of them survived, but with the bulk of their population destroyed, their numbers dwindled even further. If any are left now, they'll no doubt keep to themselves.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: However, I have heard tales of their love for redberry pie. Perhaps one of those could convince one to reveal their true heritage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just go around offering random dwarves redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well, not totally random. Apparently some of the surviving Imcando made a home for themselves near the cliffs on the Asgarnian southern peninsula. That would be a good place to start.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with this ancient axe?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: No. I don't do that anymore. I'm getting old.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, you know what to do! Get me another redberry pie and I'll have a look at that axe of yours!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like a redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I'd never say no to a redberry pie! We Imcando dwarves love them - they're GREAT!

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: By Guthix! THAT was good pie! Anyone who makes pie like THAT has got to be alright!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with this ancient axe?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: You make excellent redberry pies, [player name], so how can I refuse? Let me see what's wrong with it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here... the blade of this axe has become extremely dull and brittle. I need it restored to its former glory.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Its former glory eh? May I ask where exactly you got this axe from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was given to me by someone in Keldagrim. I prefer not to say any more than that.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Ah, Keldagrim. We have not been there for a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Hand me the axe and I'll fix it right up for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you need any materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't want any blurite this time?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Oh no, blurite is not appropriate here. No no, this is going to be a very delicate piece of work, requiring only a small amount in the way of raw materials.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Can you get me one bar of iron? Then I'll be able to start my work on this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got one right here.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Good, good! Hand me the axe and I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Here's your axe, [player name]. It looks like there's some damage to the hilt as well, probably some missing sapphires. But I'm sure you can take care of that yourself.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Here you go, [player name], good as new.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help Thurgo!

sections.title: Using sapphires on the axe before having Thurgo repair it
Using the sapphires on the axe after having Thurgo repair it
Trying to wield the restored axe
The head of a dwarf
Talking to Blasidar the sculptor before giving the items to Riki

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: How are you getting along with those items, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have your boots yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have your boots!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Excellent, excellent! Go and give them to Riki over there so I can start my work on the base of the statue!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: What else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have your clothes yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I also don't have your clothes yet, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had Vermundi make these clothes!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Oh yes, Vermundi is a good woman, she's very reliable. Wonderful clothing. Give it to Riki please.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: And? The battleaxe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, no, I'm still looking for it.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Look a little harder please!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I've got it right here.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: What, that old thing? Is that a piece of rubbish you found in the garbage?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: It looks so brittle it might well fall apart at any moment. I can't base my statue on that!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Besides, from what I recall of the original axe, this doesn't even look anything like it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yes, I've got it right here!

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Good job! Riki is your man, right over there. Give it to him please!

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model before giving him anything

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be back later with all those items!

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model with two items left

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be back with the rest of the items later!

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model with one item left

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back with the last item before you know it!

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model with clothes but not other items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some clothes for you to wear.

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Thank... you.

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model with the battleaxe but not other items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this battleaxe for you.

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Thank... you.

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model with all items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I have these boots for you to wear.

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Thank... you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And look, I have some clothes as well!

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Thank... you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And finally, I have this battleaxe for you!

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Thank... you.

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model after giving all items to him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, you're looking nice in that outfit!

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Yes... I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is the job done now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: No... it isn't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Care to tell me why not?

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Talk... to Blasidar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's very tiring trying to talk to you, you know?

sections.title: Talking to Blasidar the sculptor after giving the items to Riki

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've all the items you asked me to get you. Can you make the statue now?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Yes, yes, that will do just fine. There is only one problem now, I still need to make the head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you need me to find a crown now as well?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Oh no, no no, apparantly the Consortium has decided that crowns are out of fashion. As well as kings. Well, kings have been out of fashion here for hundreds of years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is the head supposed to look like now?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: I don't think they've quite made a decision on that. You see, I think each of the directors of the Consortium fancies their own face on top of the statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But I thought the whole point was that it would look like king what's-his-face?

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Yes, the Consortium can be a little fickle like that, unfortunately.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: But can you go and see if they've made a decision yet? I can't very well continue working on this statue without knowing what the face should look like, can I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I suppose you can't. Alright, I'll check it out.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Great! In the mean time, I'll build the statue up to the neck!

sections.title: Talking to Blasidar the sculptor again

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Any word from the Consortium yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, the directors have not made a decision yet.

lines.speaker: Blasidar the sculptor
lines.text: Then please, try to persuade one of the directors to force the Consortium into a decision. I must finish this statue before I can move onto my other work.

sections.title: Talking to Riki the sculptor's model after talking to Blasidar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad I don't have to talk to you anymore!

lines.speaker: Riki the sculptor's model
lines.text: Hrm.

sections.title: Talking to a company secretary
Turning in items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've completed your task! Here is your ore!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: And not a minute too late as well. Well done.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I am happy to see that the trust we put in you has not been in vain.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] recognizes your performance.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That's good to hear, thanks.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I have to admit, I was worried for a moment, but I'm glad to see you came through for us.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Well done, well done!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: You're always reliable, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: You have done a great service to the [company name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Wonderful! I will personally oversee that it goes to the trade floor on time!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I knew you were the right human for the job!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Thanks, I will make sure the director hears of your expeditiousness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have another task for me?

sections.title: Talking to the Red Axe Secretary

lines.speaker: Red Axe Secretary
lines.text: Don't bother me!

sections.title: Talking to the Brown Engine Secretary (before 9 November 2022)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Secretary
lines.text: I am the head secretary of the Brown Engine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Secretary
lines.text: The Brown Engine. We are one of the eight major mining companies, but our standard is higher than that of all the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is that?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Secretary
lines.text: Our policy is strictly gentlemen only, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't quite see how that is good, but if you say so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And this is a good thing, how?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Secretary
lines.text: Oh, my dear, please don't take it personally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't patronise me. I've had enough of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Secretary
lines.text: The Brown Engine is a gentleman only society, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How rude.

sections.title: Talking to the Yellow Fortune Secretary (before 9 November 2022)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: I am the head secretary of the Yellow Fortune.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: The Yellow Fortune! We are the grandest of the mining companies that make up the Consortium!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is that?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: Why, because men are not allowed to work in our company, of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ha, I see what you mean!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit drastic, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: We simply consider men to be weak and inferior.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'd better go now, I don't like being insulted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: The Yellow Fortune does not employ the services of men.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Secretary
lines.text: We often find that they are too weak or too slow to be of sufficient use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You shouldn't say such things to people.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: What can I do for you, human?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Was there anything you wanted?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: What did you need of the [company name]?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Is there anything you need assistance with?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: And what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Oh, hello there [player name]! Nice to see you!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: And what can I do for you on this fine day?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Welcome back to the palace of Keldagrim, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Come in, come in! And tell us what you need!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: How may the [company name] assist you today?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Come to help us out some more, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Purple Pewter Secretary
lines.text: Oh, by the way, I really like that outfit of yours!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Have you completed your task yet? Did you get our [number] pieces of [copper ore/tin ore/clay/iron ore/silver ore/coal/gold ore/mithril ore]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Then please, hurry up before time runs out.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: You should speak directly to the director.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I'm afraid I have no more work to offer you, [player name], please speak to the director.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: There's always work to be done, yes.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Just a moment.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Yes, the [company name] always has work to do!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Yes, I think we do have something for you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Let's see... yes, yes, I do have a task for you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I believe we have some work available, yes.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Oh yes, as you know the [company name] always has work!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Ah, always looking for ways to help us!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Of course, we reserve our best tasks just for you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] is pleased to offer more work to an eager worker such as yourself, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I do believe we do, yes!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I dare say we have something for you, yes.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Naturally, my good human, naturally!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: We need [number] pieces of [copper ore/tin ore/clay/iron ore/silver ore/coal/gold ore/mithril ore] in the next [number] minutes.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That ore needs to go on the trade floor soon, [player name], please hurry back with it.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I will await your return soon, then.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The clock is ticking, please hurry back.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Thanks, it's appreciated.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] has noted your willingness to help.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Excellent, I thought you'd like this!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: You never disappoint, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] appreciates the hard work you do.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Thanks! But hurry back please, we really need that ore!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I'm happy to hear that!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That's unfortunate to hear.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That does not reflect well on you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Please, it would be appreciated if you made up your mind before you ask. I'm very busy you know.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Fair enough, maybe some other time?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I can't say I'm too pleased to hear that, to be honest.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] isn't too pleased to hear that.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Please, in that case, you're wasting my time.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Oh. Didn't know you were the type to refuse work.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That's a great shame, [player name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I suppose you have other important business to attend to?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Is everything okay? I didn't think you normally refused our tasks.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: No? That's unusual for you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to speak to the director.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: And I would grant you such a request, why?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: You need to prove yourself to the company first before such a privilege is bestowed on you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I don't see any reason to grant such a privilege to a lowly worker. No, first you must prove yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you don't understand. I've come from Blasidar the sculptor.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Blasidar does not command the [company name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... Commander Veldaban of the Black Guard has instructed me to do this!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Nor does the Black Guard. They are under the command of the Consortium, not the other way around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I prove myself?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: It's all very simple. You perform some tasks for us, help us out a little when we need something done urgently, and in reward you will rise in our estimation.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: That is not a request that I can grant you yet, [player name], even though I am impressed with you so far.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: The [company name] appreciates what you have done so far, [player name], but the director will only speak to the most loyal of followers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you don't understand. I've come from Blasidar the sculptor.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Blasidar does not command the [company name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... Commander Veldaban of the Black Guard has instructed me to do this!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I see. This sounds like a most serious matter, but I cannot bend the rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What else must I do to prove myself?

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Keep up what you're doing, [player name]! The more you help us out, the more we appreciate you!

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: I'm sure the director will be happy to talk to you once the time is right.

lines.speaker: [company name] Secretary
lines.text: Why, naturally [player name]! The director is standing right over there!

sections.title: Talking to a company director
Turning in items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've completed your task! Here are your bars!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I will get my secretary to send this to the trade floor at once!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: You never fail to satisfy, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: You are a true asset to the [company name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I am well pleased with your work, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Guthix be praised!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: The [company name] can always rely on you, [player name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Have you ever considered joining the [company name], [player name]? You would truly make a marvelous member.

sections.title: Talking to the Red Axe Director

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: I am conducting a meeting here, begone!

sections.title: Talking to the Brown Engine Director (before 9 November 2022)

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Director
lines.text: Hello, [player name], are you trying to get in touch with your female side?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Director
lines.text: Hello there! You're looking a little feminine today, [player name]!

sections.title: Talking to the Yellow Fortune Director (before 9 November 2022)

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Director
lines.text: You are looking more and more like a male these days, [player name]... be careful now!

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Director
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. You look a little masculine today!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: How did you get in here? I don't have time to talk to you!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Talk to my secretary, I don't have time to deal with you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: My secretary will deal with any inquiries you have about the [company name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I'm busy, please leave.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I'm too important to talk to the likes of you, leave.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I can't help you, go and see my secretary.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Do you mind? You're blocking my view.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Today is not a good day, can you come back tomorrow?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: My secretary is sitting just over there, thank you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: If you're looking for the market, it's downstairs.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: You are an exceptional member of the [company name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: [player name]! I've been hearing great things about you!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Come in, [player name], my secretary has been saying good things about you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: What have you come to ask of us, honourable [player name]?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Have you come to serve the [company name] again, [player name]?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Come, [player name], let us converse a while on your future with us.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: You are proving yourself a loyal member of the [company name], [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Hello again, [player name], come in!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Come in, come in my friend!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: The [company name] is pleased to see you again!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Welcome back [player name], it's great to see you again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's an honour to meet you!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I'm always happy to make time for you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: And I'm honoured to make some time for you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Don't mention it, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Almost as much an honour as it is to meet you again, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to officially join your company.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Hmm, interesting, a human joining the [company name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: But no. You may have proven yourself to my head secretary, and much appreciated that is as well, you have not fully proven yourself to me yet.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: So no, I cannot grant you this request yet, but I will certainly keep it in mind. In the mean time make sure you help out the [company name] in any way you can.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: But you have certainly proven yourself to be a most worthwhile contributor. I have not seen such dedication in all my years as a director of this company.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: It is agreed then! You are now a 'trusted associate' of the [company name]. Welcome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will not disappoint you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any more tasks for me?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Have you completed your current task yet? Did you get me my bars of [bronze/iron/steel/silver/gold/mithril]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it, sorry.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Hurry then, I don't have time for all this.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Verily, I believe I do.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I do believe so, yes.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Why, certainly.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: A moment please, let me check.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Yes, I have something appropriate for you here.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: You don't need to do this anymore, you know?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: We need [number] bars of [bronze/iron/steel/silver/gold/mithril] in the next [number] minutes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take it.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Happy to hear that!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Admirable attitude, [player name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: The [company name] appreciates what you're doing, [player name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Excellent, get to it now!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Then hurry back, [player name], I'm counting on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that, [player name], the company really needed those bars.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I don't understand, I thought you were a loyal follower?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Then why did you ask, [player name]?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: That is an unusual thing to say for you, [player name].

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Oh? I thought you liked doing this kind of work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Blasidar the sculptor has sent me.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Oh yes? This is in connection with the statue, presumably?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He says he needs to know what the head of the statue should look like now that it's no longer going to look like King Alvis.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Oh yes, Hreidmar suggested that it should be one of us. The Era of Kings is long gone, after all. Now the Consortium provides peace and above all prosperity... so the statue should reflect this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hreidmar? Who is Hreidmar?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: He is the Director of the Red Axe. Ha, that old fool quite fancies his own head up on that statue, he thinks he can get away with that just because his company is one of the largest in the Consortium.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I shouldn't tell the sculptor that he should base it on him then?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Certainly not!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: The Consortium has yet to make a decision... all the companies are vying for the honour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would support you.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Ah, would you now, would you?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: I'm not altogether sure... much appreciated though you are, you are not really part of the [company name]. Not as such.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I were?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Were what? A part of the [company name]?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Yes, that might certainly change things...

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: This might well work. Yes, an outsider to the city might have considerably more influence than one might expect, if we play our cards right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean, play our cards right?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: We wait until the most opportune moment for you to show your support. Yes, you must really come along to one of our meetings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now you're really losing me... what meetings?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: A Consortium meeting, when all the directors meet on the top floor of the palace. We have them from time to time. We'll have another one soon, to once and for all solve our little problem with the statue.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: So what do you say, will you speak out for me at the next meeting, so that they will base the head of the statue on me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Long live the [company name]!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Hurrah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to have a think about that.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Understandable, yes. Just be sure not to think about it for too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you lot figure it out soon. This little job is starting to tire me out.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Don't count on it being any time soon. We're rather locked in an impasse on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just my luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for your time.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Not at all, it was a delight to speak to you once more!

sections.title: Art by committee
Talking to Commander Veldaban

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: What news from the Consortium, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have told the director of the [company name] that I will support their bid to be put upon the statue.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Then perhaps they can finally end their impasse! Well, let's go to the meeting then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, now?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Well... not exactly now, no. I believe the Consortium has some other important business to attend to first. Come back in a minute or so.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Yes, now! Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: I do have to warn you, this meeting may take a while. Do you want to attend the meeting personally, or do you simply want the minutes of the meetings so you can read what happened afterwards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to see the meeting.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Happy to hear that! Let's hurry then, I think the meeting is already starting!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I was mistaken. The meeting hasn't started yet, we need to wait a bit longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do NOT want to attend, I don't have time to waste.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: [player name], are you absolutely sure? You might not get another chance like this. Are you sure you just want to read the summary of the meeting instead of seeing the actual meeting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thought, I can spare a few minutes to attend.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Happy to hear that! Let's hurry then, I think the meeting is already starting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just have a lot of smithing to do today, I can't attend.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Very well. I'll give you the meeting minutes after it's finished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need more time.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Very well then. Come back when you're ready, but hurry! You don't want to miss the meeting.

sections.title: The Consortium Meeting
Prior to 9 November 2022

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Director
lines.text: Clearly it is I who is most suitable.

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Director
lines.text: A male? How preposterous!

lines.speaker: Silver Cog Director
lines.text: But the statue is clearly male! It can't have a female head!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Enough of this, everyone! This bickering is pointless.

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Director
lines.text: Clearly it is I who is most suitable.

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Director
lines.text: You? How preposterous! Evidently, it should be my head atop that statue!

lines.speaker: Silver Cog Director
lines.text: Surely, your claim to the statue can be no better than anyone else's?

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Enough of this, everyone! This bickering is pointless.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: The Red Axe is the largest and wealthiest mining company, we have all rights here!

lines.speaker: White Chisel Director
lines.text: The fact that The Red Axe does its accounting behind closed doors makes that claim quite dubious!

lines.speaker: Blue Opal Director
lines.text: We are not assembled here to squabble over accounting details or the economic state of the Consortium.

lines.speaker: White Chisel Director
lines.text: Dondakan's breaking of his rock, and the finding of his gold, has laid doubt to that claim!

lines.speaker: Blue Opal Director
lines.text: It is not yet clear what consequences that discovery will have on the economy.

lines.speaker: Green Gemstone Director
lines.text: Perhaps, then, we should choose a director for the statue at random?

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: No! I will go on top of that statue! I proposed this in the first place!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Veldaban, Commander of the Black Guard in Keldagrim, presenting himself to the Consortium with a guest.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: A human, at a Consortium meeting? What is this?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: This human helped to rebuild the statue. [He has deserved/She has deserved/They deserve] this honour, surely?

lines.speaker: Purple Pewter Director
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Green Gemstone Director
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Silver Cog Director
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Purple Pewter Director
lines.text: Well then... instead of flipping a coin, why don't we let the human decide?

lines.speaker: Brown Engine Director
lines.text: Amusing, I accept!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: This is outrageous! I'm warning you, The Red Axe will boycott the Trade Octagon!

lines.speaker: Yellow Fortune Director
lines.text: Stop your hollow threats and let the human speak.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Well then, [player name], what is it going to be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I choose...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The [chosen company name]!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: You'll regret this, all of you!
